danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming bullets and rockets from hostile aircraft and ground installations
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; armor depletion imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Military structures, turrets, and defensive emplacements on ground
danger_signals.meaning: Stationary threats that require destruction or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy helicopters and aircraft in airspace
danger_signals.meaning: Mobile aerial threats requiring evasion or engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel gauge meter on HUD declining over mission duration
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity; craft will crash if fuel reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: Armor meter on HUD decreasing with enemy hits; visual damage effects on aircraft
danger_signals.meaning: Structural integrity compromised; craft destruction imminent at zero armor

danger_signals.visual_form: Giant mirror arrays and energy weapon discharge effects
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic threat to civilian population and infrastructure

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated marked areas where helicopter can land and refuel
safety_signals.meaning: Safe extraction point for rescued civilians and hostages

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuel barrels, ammo crates, armor replenishment items scattered throughout levels
Civilians and allies picked up during missions
safety_signals.meaning: Airlifting them to landing zones restores armor and mission progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Mission areas marked as cleared after destruction or rescue completion
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward mission success; reduced threat in cleared areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player controls Strike C.O.R.E. helicopter units (Mohican, Blackhawke); operates under military/government authority
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Headquarters locations; mission briefing areas; civilian rescue points

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy aircraft, ground installations, armed personnel, private military forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Superweapon facilities (Hawaii, Las Vegas); media empire installations; construction sites for weapon components; urban strongholds

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile ground troops, defensive turrets, enemy helicopters, assassins
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortified positions in urban environments; Mexico-based operations; San Francisco installations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mission briefing screens showing objectives, target locations, and strategic information
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player receives detailed mission parameters before helicopter deployment

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active combat operations - Malone's plans progressing
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Deployed military installations, enemy aircraft presence, fortified structures across multiple North American locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively engage player; defensive positions are heavily armed; time pressure evident through fuel depletion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant mirror arrays visible in Hawaii; energy weapon discharge effects; destruction in New York City
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission objectives shift to emergency response; civilian rescue becomes critical; weapon location tracking activated

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation - Las Vegas facility breach
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-security superweapon facility with maximum defensive installations; Malone's final stronghold
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Intense combat scenario; boss-level encounter with Malone; time-bomb threat mechanic introduced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescued hostages and civilians present at landing zones; armor restoration feedback
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Successful airlifting of rescues restores craft integrity; mission progress advances

environmental_storytelling.example: Campaign spans Hawaii → Mexico → San Francisco → New York → Las Vegas, tracking both Malone's weapon component distribution and escalating threat level
Superweapon destruction of New York City serves as visible proof of Malone's capability and desperate escalation moment
Rescue of plastic surgeon in Hawaii reveals Malone's true identity (Carlos Ortega, presumed dead from prior game), indicating conspiracy and deception

environmental_storytelling.technique: Destruction of installations shows power consolidation
environmental_storytelling.example: Fortified positions across multiple cities demonstrate Malone's infiltration of legitimate U.S. infrastructure
Agent Ego's car-bomb death early in narrative establishes Malone's intelligence network penetration and ruthlessness

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reduced maneuverability, visual damage effects, armor meter depletion feedback
Urgency indicators on HUD; craft will crash if fuel reaches zero; fuel gauge visual prominence increases
Return fire toward player; attempt evasive maneuvers; organized squadrons patrol designated airspace
Turrets track and fire upon player aircraft; mines and defensive weapons activate upon proximity
Require rescue via helicopter pickup; successful rescue provides armor restoration and mission credit
Attempts detonation of chest-mounted bomb; forced onto superweapon as destruction mechanism
Grant armor restoration bonus upon successful delivery to landing zones; visible in helicopter until dropoff

notes: Urban Strike employs a modern-day political thriller narrative framework where world-state is communicated through geographic escalation, infrastructure destruction, and NPC revelations. Danger is primarily conveyed through mechanical feedback (armor/fuel meters) and environmental threat density. Factions are distinguished through mission objectives and opposing force deployment patterns rather than explicit visual markers (colors/symbols not documented in available sources). The game's manifestation layer emphasizes resource scarcity (fuel, ammo, armor) as primary tension vectors, with civilian rescue mechanics providing counterbalance to combat danger. Political stakes are revealed narratively rather than through visual world-state indicators.
